stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating its use in generative AI tasks.
Key associated technologies include transformers and safetensors, foundational elements of modern LLMs.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
Indicates the model is available for text generation tasks.
Tags confirm its relevance within the transformers and LLM ecosystem.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the 'deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1' model on Hugging Face.
The model is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI pipelines.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors, indicating a focus on model deployment and benchmarking.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated MLX for enhanced performance.
This update specifically targets and optimizes for Apple Silicon hardware.
The feature is currently available in a preview release.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It connects these apps to local AI coding agents.
A key feature is that all AI processing runs entirely on the user's local device.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to streamline the setup and execution of coding tools.
It enables running various coding models (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) both locally and in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification.
The models, available in 20B and 120B sizes, are being brought to users via a partnership with OpenAI and ROOST.
The models are permissively licensed under the Apache 2.0 license, promoting open-source safety tooling.

stories.title: Code as Agent Harness
stories.author: Jingrui He
bullet_points: Code is evolving from just an output of LLMs to an operational substrate for agent reasoning and action.
The concept of 'agent harnesses' is introduced to unify the understanding of how code functions within emerging agentic systems.
This shift impacts how LLMs are used in software engineering, moving beyond simple code generation to complex execution-based verification.

stories.title: ESI-Bench: Towards Embodied Spatial Intelligence that Closes the Perception-Action Loop
stories.author: Yejin Choi
bullet_points: Introduces ESI-Bench, a new benchmark focused on Embodied Spatial Intelligence.
Proposes moving beyond passive sensing by recasting the observer as an active actor in a perception-action loop.
Aims to enable agents to actively uncover unseen information like occluded structure and dynamics.

stories.title: Predictable Confabulations: Factual Recall by LLMs Scales with Model Size and Topic Frequency
stories.author: Tegawendé F. Bissyandé
bullet_points: The research establishes a novel scaling law linking factual recall in LLMs to both model size and topic frequency in training data.
Factual recall quality exhibits a sigmoid relationship when combining model parameter count and topic representation.
The study analyzed 38 models against over 8,900 scholarly references, showing these two factors explain 60% of variance across tested models.

stories.title: ESI-Bench: Towards Embodied Spatial Intelligence that Closes the Perception-Action Loop
stories.author: Yejin Choi
bullet_points: Introduces ESI-Bench, a new benchmark focusing on Embodied Spatial Intelligence.
Proposes moving beyond passive sensing by recasting the observer as an active actor in a perception-action loop.
Aims to allow agents to actively uncover unseen information, such as occluded structure and dynamics.

stories.title: Predictable Confabulations: Factual Recall by LLMs Scales with Model Size and Topic Frequency
stories.author: Tegawendé F. Bissyandé
bullet_points: The research establishes a new scaling law linking factual recall in LLMs to both model size and topic frequency in training data.
Factual recall quality exhibits a sigmoid relationship based on the log-linear combination of model parameters and topic representation.
The study analyzed 38 models across over 8,900 scholarly references, showing these two factors explain 60% of variance in dense models.

stories.title: EnvFactory: Scaling Tool-Use Agents via Executable Environments Synthesis and Robust RL
stories.author: Zhijiang Guo
bullet_points: Introduces 'EnvFactory' to address scalability and robustness issues in training LLM tool-use agents via Agentic RL.
Overcomes limitations of current methods, such as reliance on costly real-world APIs or hallucination-prone simulators.
Focuses on synthesizing executable environments to generate more realistic and diverse training data for complex agent reasoning.

stories.title: Code as Agent Harness
stories.author: Jingrui He
bullet_points: LLMs are evolving beyond just code generation to becoming integral parts of agentic systems.
Code is increasingly functioning as an 'operational substrate' for agent reasoning, action, and environment modeling.
The paper introduces the concept of 'code as agent harness' to unify this new role of code in AI agents.

stories.title: Predictable Confabulations: Factual Recall by LLMs Scales with Model Size and Topic Frequency
stories.author: Tegawendé F. Bissyandé
bullet_points: The research establishes a new scaling law linking factual recall in LLMs to both model size and the frequency of topics in the training data.
Factual recall quality was found to follow a sigmoid relationship based on the log-linear combination of model parameters and topic representation.
The study analyzed 38 models across over 8,900 scholarly references, showing that model size and topic composition explain 60% of the variance in recall.

stories.title: Generative AI Advertising as a Problem of Trustworthy Commercial Intervention
stories.author: Qiaozhu Mei
bullet_points: Generative AI is shifting advertising from discrete placements to interventions within the LLM generation process.
Current systems maintain a visible boundary between ads and AI responses, but this is often bypassed in practice.
The core problem identified is the lack of user detection when commercial influence is embedded subtly within AI outputs.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags like 'transformers' and 'pytorch'.
The pipeline demonstrated is 'text-generation', indicating a focus on generative AI capabilities.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now offers experimental image generation capabilities.
The feature is currently available for users on macOS.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage Claude Code functionality within the Ollama ecosystem.
It facilitates the use of powerful proprietary models alongside open-source models.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a working directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Anker fixes the two worst things about power stations
stories.author: Thomas Ricker
bullet_points: Anker's Solix S2000 addresses key drawbacks of power stations, specifically high idle power draw and large size.
The device offers a competitive price point, calculated at just $0.29 per Wh.
It represents an improvement in portable power solutions by optimizing efficiency and footprint.

stories.title: Gemini is in danger of going full Copilot
stories.author: Allison Johnson
bullet_points: The article discusses the integration and perceived over-saturation of Google's Gemini AI across various Google applications.
The core theme is a critique of Gemini's deployment strategy, suggesting it is becoming overly pervasive ('creeping').
The title draws a comparison to Microsoft's Copilot, implying a trend toward ubiquitous AI assistance.

stories.title: LG will release the first 1000Hz, 1080p gaming monitor this year
stories.author: Richard Lawler
bullet_points: LG is set to release the UltraGear 25G590B gaming monitor.
This monitor features a native 1000Hz refresh rate at 1080p resolution.
The product aims to bridge the gap between high refresh rates and usable resolution in gaming monitors.

stories.title: In addition to space stations, Vast says it will now build high-power satellites
stories.author: Eric Berger
bullet_points: Vast is expanding its business scope beyond space stations to include high-power satellite construction.
The move aligns with the industry trend of successful space companies diversifying their product offerings.
This signals a potential broadening of Vast's market presence within the commercial space sector.

stories.title: Iran demands Big Tech pay fees for undersea Internet cables in Strait of Hormuz
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: Iran is demanding fees from Big Tech companies for using undersea internet cables in the Strait of Hormuz.
This geopolitical demand is forcing US technology companies to explore alternative overland fiber routes.
The incident highlights the vulnerability of global internet infrastructure to regional political disputes.

stories.title: Elon Musk took too long to sue OpenAI, jury unanimously agrees
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: Elon Musk sued OpenAI, resulting in a jury verdict.
The jury unanimously agreed with the outcome of the lawsuit.
Musk intends to appeal the decision, which a judge immediately affirmed.

stories.title: DashAttention: Differentiable and Adaptive Sparse Hierarchical Attention
stories.author: Marcos V. Treviso
bullet_points: Introduces DashAttention, a new method for hierarchical attention in AI models.
Addresses limitations in existing methods (like NSA and InfLLMv2) where top-k selection breaks gradient flow.
Proposes a differentiable and adaptive approach to sparse hierarchical attention.

stories.title: Vision-OPD: Learning to See Fine Details for Multimodal LLMs via On-Policy Self-Distillation
stories.author: Yaojie Lu
bullet_points: Multimodal LLMs (MLLMs) exhibit a 'regional-to-global perception gap,' struggling with fine-grained visual understanding.
The model's performance improves when conditioned on evidence-centered image crops rather than the full image, indicating a focus issue.
The research proposes 'Vision-OPD,' a method using on-policy self-distillation to help MLLMs focus on relevant visual evidence.

stories.title: What Does the AI Doctor Value? Auditing Pluralism in the Clinical Ethics of Language Models
stories.author: Gabriel A. Brat
bullet_points: The paper addresses the inherent pluralism and ethical conflicts in medical practice.
It investigates how the ethical values embedded in Large Language Models (LLMs) influence medical advice.
A new framework is proposed for auditing value pluralism within the clinical ethics of AI.

stories.title: DexHoldem: Playing Texas Hold'em with Dexterous Embodied System
stories.author: Yi Ma
bullet_points: Introduces DexHoldem, a real-world system-level benchmark for evaluating embodied AI.
The benchmark uses Texas Hold'em manipulation with a ShadowHand to test complex, multi-step agent capabilities.
It moves beyond isolated skills by requiring agents to perceive, act contextually, and maintain scene usability.

stories.title: Semantic Generative Tuning for Unified Multimodal Models
stories.author: Yanwei Li
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of decoupling visual understanding and generation in Unified Multimodal Models (UMMs).
Proposes 'Semantic Generative Tuning' to unify training paradigms for better representation alignment.
Aims to enable mutual reinforcement between visual understanding and generation within a single architecture.

stories.title: Distilling Tabular Foundation Models for Structured Health Data
stories.author: Pratinav Seth
bullet_points: The research addresses the high inference cost of Tabular Foundation Models (TFMs) used in health data.
Knowledge distillation is employed to transfer the predictive power of large TFMs to smaller, lightweight models.
A novel technique, stratified out-of-fold teacher labeling, is used to prevent context leakage during distillation.

stories.title: DashAttention: Differentiable and Adaptive Sparse Hierarchical Attention
stories.author: Marcos V. Treviso
bullet_points: Introduces DashAttention, a novel method for hierarchical attention in ML models.
Addresses limitations in existing methods (like NSA/InfLLMv2) where top-k selection prevents gradient flow.
Proposes a differentiable and adaptive approach to sparse hierarchical attention.

stories.title: A Readiness-Driven Runtime for Pipeline-Parallel Training under Runtime Variability
stories.author: Wei Xu
bullet_points: Addresses a critical issue in large-model training: runtime variability in pipeline-parallel execution.
Critiques existing pipeline systems for relying on static or pre-committed execution schedules.
Proposes a 'readiness-driven runtime' to mitigate stage misalignment and idle bubbles caused by unpredictable task readiness.

stories.title: SURGE: Approximation-free Training Free Particle Filter for Diffusion Surrogate
stories.author: Yiping Lu
bullet_points: Introduces URGE, a new derivative-free inference-time scaling algorithm for diffusion models.
Addresses the computational overhead and bias introduced by existing guidance techniques in diffusion models.
Uses path-wise importance reweighting based on Girsanov Estimation to improve sample quality without repeated score evaluations.

stories.title: Vision-OPD: Learning to See Fine Details for Multimodal LLMs via On-Policy Self-Distillation
stories.author: Yaojie Lu
bullet_points: Multimodal LLMs (MLLMs) exhibit a 'regional-to-global perception gap' when handling fine-grained visual understanding.
The model performs better on evidence-centered image crops than on the full image, indicating a focus/attention issue rather than just local recognition failure.
The paper introduces 'Vision-OPD' as a method using On-Policy Self-Distillation to improve MLLM's ability to focus on decisive visual evidence.

stories.title: PIXLRelight: Controllable Relighting via Intrinsic Conditioning
stories.author: Ronald Clark
bullet_points: PIXLRelight introduces a feed-forward method for physically controllable single-image relighting.
It addresses limitations in existing methods, such as poor lighting control or error accumulation in rendering chains.
The core innovation is using a shared intrinsic conditioning to bridge Physically Based Rendering (PBR) and learned image synthesis.

stories.title: General Preference Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: John M. Cioffi
bullet_points: Current LLM alignment is split between online RL (good for verifiable tasks like math/code) and preference optimization (good for open-ended generation).
Online RL is limited by the need for a programmatic verifier, which fails on open-ended tasks.
The research identifies the need for a verifier capable of assessing open-ended quality, suggesting scalar reward models are insufficient.

stories.title: Learned Memory Attenuation in Sage-Husa Kalman Filters for Robust UAV State Estimation
stories.author: Marcin Żugaj
bullet_points: Addresses limitations of classical Kalman filters (like SHKF) in UAV state estimation due to dynamic environments and noise.
Proposes the N-Deep Recurrent Sage-Husa Filter (NDR-SHKF) to overcome the static forgetting factor compromise.
Focuses on improving robustness and balancing stability/responsiveness in real-world UAV applications.

stories.title: DashAttention: Differentiable and Adaptive Sparse Hierarchical Attention
stories.author: Marcos V. Treviso
bullet_points: Introduces DashAttention, a new model for sparse hierarchical attention.
Addresses limitations in existing methods (like NSA and InfLLMv2) where top-k selection prevents gradient flow.
Proposes a differentiable and adaptive approach to handle variable numbers of relevant tokens in hierarchical attention.

stories.title: ESI-Bench: Towards Embodied Spatial Intelligence that Closes the Perception-Action Loop
stories.author: Yejin Choi
bullet_points: Introduces ESI-Bench, a new benchmark focusing on Embodied Spatial Intelligence.
Proposes moving beyond passive sensing by recasting the observer as an active actor in a perception-action loop.
Aims to allow agents to uncover unseen information such as occluded structure, dynamics, and containment.

stories.title: Vision-OPD: Learning to See Fine Details for Multimodal LLMs via On-Policy Self-Distillation
stories.author: Yaojie Lu
bullet_points: Multimodal LLMs (MLLMs) exhibit a 'regional-to-global perception gap,' struggling with fine-grained visual understanding.
Performance improves when MLLMs are conditioned on evidence-centered image crops rather than the full image.
The research proposes 'Vision-OPD' to address this gap by learning to focus on relevant, fine-grained visual evidence.

stories.title: General Preference Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: John M. Cioffi
bullet_points: LLM alignment is currently split between two methods: online RL (good for verifiable tasks like math/code) and preference optimization (good for open-ended generation).
Online RL is limited by the need for programmatic verifiers, which cannot handle open-ended tasks.
The paper suggests the key challenge is developing a verifier capable of assessing open-ended quality, noting that a simple scalar reward model is insufficient.

stories.title: EnvFactory: Scaling Tool-Use Agents via Executable Environments Synthesis and Robust RL
stories.author: Zhijiang Guo
bullet_points: Addresses the bottleneck in scaling LLM tool-use agents by focusing on robust execution environments.
Proposes 'EnvFactory' to synthesize scalable and realistic training environments, moving beyond costly real-world APIs.
Tackles the scarcity of realistic training data by improving upon limited or over-specified synthetic trajectories.

stories.title: GIM: Evaluating models via tasks that integrate multiple cognitive domains
stories.author: Steven McClain
bullet_points: Introduces GIM (Grounded Integration Measure), a new benchmark for evaluating Large Language Models (LLMs).
GIM addresses limitations in current benchmarks (like GPQA and ARC-AGI) by moving beyond simple knowledge recall or purely abstract reasoning.
The benchmark focuses on tasks that require integrating multiple cognitive domains, aiming for a more holistic assessment of model capability.

stories.title: I red-teamed Oracle APEX 26.1's new AI Agent feature in the 72 hours after it went GA. Claude refused 7 of my 10 attacks on its own.
stories.author: rkondoju
bullet_points: The article details a red-teaming exercise performed on Oracle APEX 26.1's new AI Agent feature.
The testing involved mapping 10 attack patterns against Anthropic Claude Sonnet 4.6.
The analysis identified specific attack classes that succeeded despite Claude's defenses and highlighted a vulnerability in APEX 26.1.
